title: [InstCombine] Generalize and consolidate phi translation check

tests.source_program:
define i32 @phi_operand_same_block_as_condition_phi(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, i32 %val1, i32 %val2) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %merge

bb2:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi_cond = phi i1 [ %c1, %bb1 ], [ %c2, %bb2 ]
  %phi_val = phi i32 [ %val1, %bb1 ], [ %val2, %bb2 ]
  %result = select i1 %phi_cond, i32 %phi_val, i32 0
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i32 @phi_operand_same_block_as_condition_phi(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, i32 %val1, i32 %val2) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %merge

bb2:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %result = phi i32 [ %val1, %bb1 ], [ 0, %bb2 ]
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions phi nodes in same block are allowed but provides no concrete test case. This tests select with phi operand in same block as condition phi, which the old canSelectOperandBeMappingIntoPredBlock allowed but wasn't explicitly tested.

define i32 @phi_operand_same_block_as_condition_phi(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, i32 %val1, i32 %val2) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:                                              ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

bb2:                                              ; preds = %entry
  %0 = select i1 %c2, i32 %val2, i32 0
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %bb2, %bb1
  %phi_cond = phi i32 [ %val1, %bb1 ], [ %0, %bb2 ]
  ret i32 %phi_cond
}

tests.source_program:
define i32 @select_in_successor_block_to_phi(i1 %c1, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %phi_block

bb2:
  br label %phi_block

phi_block:
  %phi_cond = phi i1 [ true, %bb1 ], [ false, %bb2 ]
  br label %select_block

select_block:
  %result = select i1 %phi_cond, i32 %a, i32 %b
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i32 @select_in_successor_block_to_phi(i1 %c1, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %phi_block

bb2:
  br label %phi_block

phi_block:
  %result = phi i32 [ %a, %bb1 ], [ %b, %bb2 ]
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Old code required SI.getParent() == CondPHI.getParent() for cross-block operands. This test has select in a successor block to the phi, which the old conservative filter would reject but the new dominance-based check accepts since %a and %b dominate the phi block.

define i32 @select_in_successor_block_to_phi(i1 %c1, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %phi_block

bb2:
  br label %phi_block

phi_block:
  %phi_cond = phi i1 [ true, %bb1 ], [ false, %bb2 ]
  br label %select_block

select_block:
  %result = select i1 %phi_cond, i32 %a, i32 %b
  ret i32 %result
}

tests.source_program:
define i32 @mixed_constant_and_dominating_instruction_operands(i1 %c1, ptr %p) {
entry:
  %load = load i32, ptr %p
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %merge

bb2:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi_cond = phi i1 [ true, %bb1 ], [ false, %bb2 ]
  %result = select i1 %phi_cond, i32 %load, i32 100
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i32 @mixed_constant_and_dominating_instruction_operands(i1 %c1, ptr %p) {
entry:
  %load = load i32, ptr %p
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %merge

bb2:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %result = phi i32 [ %load, %bb1 ], [ 100, %bb2 ]
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions constants and dominating instructions separately but doesn't test them mixed together. This validates the generalized check handles heterogeneous operand types (one dominating instruction, one constant) in a single select, which the old code's binary pass/fail filter would handle less elegantly.

alive2.src:
define i32 @mixed_constant_and_dominating_instruction_operands(i1 %c1, ptr %p) {
entry:
  %load = load i32, ptr %p
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %merge

bb2:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi_cond = phi i1 [ true, %bb1 ], [ false, %bb2 ]
  %result = select i1 %phi_cond, i32 %load, i32 100
  ret i32 %result
}

  br label %merge

bb2:                                              ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %bb2, %bb1
  %result = phi i32 [ %load, %bb1 ], [ 100, %bb2 ]
  ret i32 %result
}
